anchorText: and He assigns All thy tears over, like pure2 crystallines2, For younger fellow-workers of the soil To wear for amulets.

levels.body: This passage refers to two verses of the Bible: firstly, Revelation 21:4: "And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes; and there shall be no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain: for the former things are passed away" (*King James Version*). Secondly, a few verses below these in Revelation 21:11: "Having the glory of God: and her light was like unto a stone most precious, even like a jasper stone, clear as crystal."
worksCited: *King James Version*. Bible Gateway, www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+21%3A4&version=KJV. Accessed 19. Jul. 2019.

levels.body: In Rev 21:11 it is the crystal-clear light in which Jerusalem appears having descended directly "out of heaven from God" (*King James Version* Rev. 21:10). However, the connection between the crystal (cf. [[crystallines]]) and God´s grace may be established similarly in the poem: With his grace, God transforms the [[tears]] into [[amulets]] protecting those who wear it, the "fellow-workers of the soil" (8), from evil so that there is "neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there be any more pain" (Rev 21:4) among those who dutifully do their [[work]].
worksCited: *King James Version*. Bible Gateway, www.biblegateway.com/passage/?search=Revelation+21%3A4&version=KJV. Accessed 19. Jul. 2019.
